name: bun:sqlite Synchronous Pattern
description: bun:sqlite is synchronous. Use Database class from 'bun:sqlite'. Enable WAL mode: db.exec('PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL'). Set busy_timeout: db.exec('PRAGMA busy_timeout=5000'). Use db.prepare() for prepared statements. db.query().all() for SELECT, db.run() for INSERT/UPDATE. No async/await needed for DB ops.

content: All JSON files written via Bun.write must include a trailing newline: `${JSON.stringify(data, null, '\t')}\n`. Biome enforces trailing newlines, and omitting them causes lint failures on generated/runtime files like sessions.json. Apply consistently across all write sites — grep for JSON.stringify to find them all.

description: bun:test mock.module() is process-global and leaks across all test files run in the same bun test invocation. Mocking a module like ../src/config.ts in one test file causes other test files that import the same module to get the mocked version, causing 73+ test failures.
resolution: Avoid mock.module entirely; instead extract testable pure functions or use spyOn for instance methods.

name: macOS temp dir symlink resolution for git worktree tests
description: macOS temp dir symlink: /var/folders -> /private/var/folders. Git resolves symlinks in worktree paths, so when creating temp git repos for tests, wrap the path with realpathSync() to avoid path comparison mismatches. This affects listWorktrees output and any path-based lookups like removeWorktree branch resolution.

name: bd CLI JSON normalization
description: bd show --json returns an array (not single object), and uses 'issue_type' field name instead of 'type'. Client must unwrap array for show() and map issue_type->type via normalizeIssue(). Integration tests exposed these mismatches that mocks hid.

name: Bun test.skipIf needs synchronous check
description: bun:test test.skipIf(condition) evaluates condition at test registration time (synchronously), not at runtime. Use Bun.spawnSync for CLI availability checks, not Bun.spawn in beforeAll. beforeAll runs after test registration, so async availability checks are too late for skipIf.

name: Selective Bun.spawn spy for testing
description: Save original Bun.spawn before spying, define a selectiveMock function with (...args: unknown[]): unknown signature that branches on cmd[0], then cast with mockImplementation(selectiveMock as typeof Bun.spawn). This avoids TypeScript overload errors while allowing real subprocess calls to pass through.

name: Delete/modify conflicts for testing tier escalation
description: To test resolver tiers beyond auto-resolve with real git: create a delete/modify conflict (delete file on one branch, modify on other). Git reports it as unmerged but produces NO conflict markers in the working copy, causing resolveConflictsKeepIncoming to return null. This naturally escalates past Tier 2. For Tier 4 (reimagine), use a separate file in entry.filesModified that exists on both branches, since git show fails for deleted files.

name: Template clone for test repos
description: Cache a template git repo on first call (lazy singleton), then use git clone for subsequent repos (1 subprocess instead of 5). Avoid --local flag on macOS — hardlinks trigger EFAULT in Bun rm(). Use GIT_AUTHOR/COMMITTER env vars instead of per-repo git config to eliminate 2 more subprocess spawns.
git init without -b main causes branch name mismatches between macOS (main) and Linux CI (master).
resolution: Always pass -b main to git init in test helpers to ensure consistent branch naming across platforms.

name: test-infinite-loop-isolation
description: When testing commands that start infinite loops (like dashboard polling), use process.chdir to a temp dir WITHOUT required config files so initialization throws BEFORE the loop starts. This proves validation passed while preventing background loops from leaking across test files. Promise.race abandons promises but doesn't stop running code.
Dashboard tests starting infinite poll loops via Promise.race leak loops after test completes. Abandoned promise's loop continues writing to stdout, polluting subsequent test files.
resolution: chdir to a temp dir without .overstory/config.yaml so loadConfig() throws before entering the while loop. Prevents infinite loop from ever starting.

name: ci-git-identity-config
description: CI test repos need repo-level git config (user.name/email) because resolver's runGit doesn't pass GIT_TEST_ENV and CI runners lack global git identity. Fix: set config in createTempGitRepo().

name: bun-spy-mock-clear
description: bun v1.3.9+ test reporter may flush output through console.log/error between spy creation and test action, inflating call counts. Always call mockClear() after spyOn() before the assertion-triggering action.

'Never mock what you can use for real' has a critical exception: tests calling killAllTmuxSessions() or other global-state-mutating functions MUST mock those operations. Real tmux kills during bun test destroyed a live agent swarm.
resolution: The philosophy applies to isolated resources (temp dirs, :memory: DBs) but NOT to shared global resources (tmux server, process table). Add tmux mocking to clean.test.ts.

name: pathExists helper
description: Use stat() from node:fs/promises for both files and directories. Bun.file().exists() only works for files, not directories. Pattern: async function pathExists(path: string): Promise<boolean> { try { await stat(path); return true; } catch { return false; } }

name: Path normalization for symlinks
description: When comparing file paths from different sources (git worktree list, SessionStore, file system), use realpathSync() to normalize symlinks before comparison. On macOS, /tmp -> /private/tmp causes mismatches without normalization. Wrap in try/catch since realpathSync throws for non-existent paths.

content: Test git repo setup: Always disable GPG signing in test git repos with 'git config commit.gpgsign false' to prevent test failures from GPG configuration.

Mail test pattern for repeated checks: When testing mail check multiple times, must send new messages between checks since check/checkInject mark messages as read. Use separate client.send() calls to create fresh unread messages for each test assertion.

name: Dependency Injection for Test Isolation
description: Instead of mock.module() (which leaks across tests), define optional deps parameter on functions and pass mock implementations. Example: checkConsistency(config, dir, deps?) where deps = {listSessions, isProcessAlive}. Tests create mocks and pass via deps param. This prevents module-level mock leakage.

agents.test.ts had process.chdir(tempDir) in beforeEach but never restored originalCwd in afterEach, leaving the Bun test process with a deleted temp dir as cwd. This poisoned subsequent test files in the same worker — 6 tests in prime.test.ts and color.test.ts failed only in CI due to test file ordering.
resolution: Always save and restore originalCwd when using process.chdir in tests. Subprocess tests using cwd: process.cwd() with relative imports are fragile — use absolute paths derived from import.meta.dir instead. These bugs are invisible locally since they depend on Bun test file ordering.

content: When adding a required field to OverstoryConfig, ALL test fixtures across the codebase (src/agents/manifest.test.ts, src/doctor/*.test.ts) must be updated to include the new field — this is a cross-cutting concern that affects files outside any single builder's scope. Consider making new OverstoryConfig fields optional or assigning fixture updates to a separate task when doing batch coordination.
Biome cannot lint files in .overstory/worktrees/* due to the !**/.overstory exclusion in biome.json. This is expected — run tsc typecheck and bun test as the quality gates for worktree agents.

AgentRuntime interface extension pattern: add new capabilities as optional fields (readonly headless?, buildDirectSpawn?, parseEvents?) to keep backward compatibility with all existing runtime adapters. New supporting types (DirectSpawnOpts, AgentEvent) go in the same file before the interface definition, under their own section comment.

SaplingRuntime test pattern: use real temp dirs (mkdtemp), deployConfig calls write both SAPLING.md and .sapling/guards.json, guards are parsed via JSON.parse for assertion, parseTranscript uses first-model-wins (!model guard) unlike Codex which uses last-model-wins. biome check must run from canonical repo root (npx biome check <worktree-path>) not via bun run lint in worktrees.

SaplingRuntime model alias fix pattern (overstory-28ae): buildEnv must forward ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_*_MODEL env vars from model.env so buildDirectSpawn can resolve gateway-routed aliases. Add loop: for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(providerEnv)) { if (key.startsWith('ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_') && key.endsWith('_MODEL')) env[key] = value; }. buildDirectSpawn fallback map (SAPLING_ALIAS_FALLBACKS) resolves bare aliases (haiku/sonnet/opus) to concrete model IDs when no gateway env var is present.

DirectSpawnOpts.model optional pattern (overstory-7110): making model?: string optional in DirectSpawnOpts allows runtimes to conditionally include --model. When undefined, runtime omits --model so agent uses its own config. Safe for runtimes that have their own model config (like sapling). Existing toEqual tests on ResolvedModel need isExplicitOverride added when field is included in all return paths.

review/types.ts self-contained module pattern: all review types live in src/review/types.ts (not src/types.ts), mirroring src/health/types.ts. ReviewDimension and ReviewSubjectType are union types. DimensionScore, ReviewRecord, StalenessState, ReviewSummary, InsertReviewRecord are interfaces. reviewerSource is literal type 'deterministic'.

RunStatus stopped extension pattern (overstory-885f): adding a new terminal RunStatus value requires: (1) extend type in types.ts, (2) update RunStore.completeRun signature, (3) update CREATE_RUNS_TABLE CHECK, (4) add migrateRunsStatusStopped to rebuild table via explicit-column INSERT...SELECT to avoid column-order bugs when ALTER TABLE was previously used

SQLite CHECK constraint table rebuild pattern: when a CHECK constraint must be extended (e.g., new enum value), SQLite requires full table rebuild. Detect via sqlite_master sql column. Use explicit column names in INSERT...SELECT (not SELECT *) to avoid position-mismatch bugs when ALTER TABLE was used to add columns previously.

MissionStore.bindSessions SQLite COALESCE pattern (overstory-3819): bindSessions uses COALESCE(, analyst_session_id) so passing null preserves existing value — partial binding is safe and idempotent. bindSessions prepared stmt has , , ,  params.

Bun.Glob.scan directory existence check (overstory-cf55): Bun.file(dir).exists() returns false for directories. Use stat() from node:fs/promises to check if a directory exists before calling Bun.Glob scan. Wrap in try/catch to return [] on ENOENT.
MissionStore.completeMission(id) atomically sets state=completed AND completed_at in one SQL UPDATE. Renamed from complete() to completeMission() to avoid ambiguity with other store close() methods
MissionStore.list() uses 4 parameterized query variants (hasState x hasLimit) to avoid string-interpolated LIMIT. All WHERE/LIMIT clauses use SQLite named params ($state, $limit)

PersistentAgentTmuxDeps / CaptureDeps DI mock pattern: in persistent-root.test.ts, mock PersistentAgentTmuxDeps with makeTmuxDeps(overrides) helper returning default no-op async functions; use createSessionStore(dbPath) to pre-populate sessions before calling lifecycle functions; test teardown uses rm() in afterEach

bun test SIGTERM workaround: running bun test src/events/tailer.test.ts or bun test (no args) exits with SIGTERM (143) in .overstory/worktrees — pre-existing issue. Run tests per-directory or specify explicit test files.

roles.test.ts MissionStore mock pattern: makeStoreWithSpy() must include getById returning a truthy object ({id}) so happy-path tests pass the mission existence check in roles.ts. Edge-case tests (missing mission) set getById to return null, not bindSessions throwing.

review/batching.ts self-contained batching module pattern (overstory-d869): ReviewBatch, EvidenceEntry, ConcernType all defined locally in the module (not in src/types.ts), per review types self-contained convention. assembleBatch() and batchForReview() are synchronous, grouping evidence from ReviewStore and EventStore. Error-pattern grouping uses toolName (not eventType) because EventType is a narrow union — all errors share eventType='error'.

Optional field roundtrip pattern for SQLite: when adding an optional field (promptVersion?: string | null) to an interface that roundtrips through SQLite, use conditional spread in rowToSession: '...(row.col \!== null ? { fieldName: row.col } : {})'. This preserves undefined semantics for toEqual() tests — stores that don't set the field get undefined (not null) back, matching original objects.

schema-consistency.test.ts must be updated when adding SQLite columns (overstory-bdbe): src/schema-consistency.test.ts has hardcoded expected column lists for each table. When adding a column to sessions/store.ts CREATE_TABLE, you must also add the column name to the expected array in schema-consistency.test.ts. This file is typically not in builder scope — request scope extension from parent.

PROFILE_INSTRUCTIONS placeholder pattern (overstory-1736): OverlayConfig.profileContent?: string field holds Layer 2 (deployment profile) content. formatProfile() in overlay.ts is a pass-through — the caller (canopy) owns formatting. {{PROFILE_INSTRUCTIONS}} placeholder in overlay.md.tmpl sits between {{BASE_DEFINITION}} and the assignment section, creating the three-layer HOW+WHAT_KIND+WHAT system.

Session state split across 3 layers: SQLite (live sessions + runs), JSON (checkpoints per handoff), YAML (agent identity CVs). Sessions table has 26 columns with WAL mode for concurrent access; agent_name is unique key and linking reference. Checkpoint is ephemeral snapshot saved before handoff only — not auto-saved on completion. Identity updates additive (sessionsCompleted counter, deduplicated expertiseDomains, FIFO recentTasks capped at 20).

recovery/types.ts self-contained module pattern: all recovery types live in src/recovery/types.ts — WorktreeStatus, SwarmSnapshot, RecoveryBundleManifest, ComponentRestoreStatus, ReconciliationStatus, ReconciliationReport, SnapshotOptions, RestoreOptions. Imports AgentIdentity/AgentSession/MailMessage/MergeEntry/Mission/Run/SessionCheckpoint/SessionHandoff from ../types.ts

SnapshotError/RecoveryError error class pattern (overstory-a54c): SnapshotError has snapshotId: string | null context, code SNAPSHOT_ERROR; RecoveryError has bundleId: string | null context, code RECOVERY_ERROR. Both extend OverstoryError, added before LifecycleError in src/errors.ts

snapshot.ts collector pattern (overstory-5834): createSnapshot opens SQLite stores defensively (existsSync before factory), uses createRunStore separately from createSessionStore (both share sessions.db), closes each store in finally blocks, collects file-based state (checkpoints/handoffs/identities) in parallel via Promise.all, exportSnapshotBundle writes manifest.json last as atomicity signal. Trailing newline on all JSON files via ${JSON.stringify(data, null, '\t')}\n.

restore.ts + reconcile.ts DI pattern for tmux: ReconcileDeps interface with checkTmuxSession injectable — lets tests mock tmux without running it, following PersistentAgentTmuxDeps pattern. Same shape: interface with async fn, defaultDeps constant, optional param on exported function.

reconcile.ts component status rules: tmux alive AND worktree exists = restored; both gone = missing (operator re-spawn action); only one gone = degraded (operator inspect action). Overall status: all restored/skipped = restored, else = partial. No 'failed' status returned from reconcile — failures surface as missing/degraded per agent.

restore.ts store insert strategy (overstory-225e): use store factory to init schema+WAL, then call insert methods per record with try/catch for conflicts. Sessions use upsert (safe). Runs use createRun + catch. Mail uses insert({id, from, to, ...}) + catch — omitting delivery-state fields (they reset to defaults). merge-queue re-enqueues pending entries only (not merged/failed). Missions use create() + updateState/updatePhase for non-defaults.

When adding new commands to src/index.ts, follow 3-step pattern: (1) import in alphabetical order near line 13-54, (2) add command name to COMMANDS array in alphabetical position, (3) add program.addCommand(create*Command()) near line 446 before unknown command handler. Typecheck will fail until the referenced command files exist — this is expected when index.ts is built in parallel with the command files.

agent-env e2e test pattern (overstory-af3c): guard tests must include OVERSTORY_RUNTIME_SESSION_ID in both the env passed to Bun.spawn AND in writeAgentEnvFile calls. Define TEST_SESSION_ID constant. removeAgentEnvFile tests must check session-scoped path (.agent-env.$SESSION_ID) not legacy singleton.

resilience/types.ts self-contained module pattern (overstory-f949): all resilience types live in src/resilience/types.ts — RetryConfig, CircuitBreakerConfig, RerouteConfig, ResilienceConfig (config types) plus CircuitBreakerState, RetryRecord, RerouteDecision (state/record types)

resilience config extension pattern (overstory-f949): OverstoryConfig.resilience? uses Partial<ResilienceConfig[sub]> for each sub-section — defaults fill missing fields; DEFAULT_CONFIG includes full resilience defaults; KNOWN_FIELDS extended with resilience/resilienceRetry/resilienceCircuitBreaker/resilienceReroute keys; validateConfig and validateUnknownFields both extended for resilience

Health resilience dimension pattern (overstory-fb4b): adding 7th factor to health scoring requires: (1) 3 new fields in HealthSignals (openBreakerCount, activeRetryCount, recentRerouteCount), (2) resilience signal collection in signals.ts using createResilienceStore — use listOpenBreakers().filter(b=>b.state==='open') and getPendingRetries(1000) (not getActiveRetries which doesn't exist), (3) rebalanced FACTOR_WEIGHTS summing to 1.0, (4) scoreResilience() function, (5) rule in RULES array in recommendations.ts, (6) update all healthySignals() helpers in test files.

swap.ts SwapReason + RerouteOptions pattern (overstory-f804): SwapReason = 'rate_limit_swap' | 'failure_reroute'; RerouteOptions extends SwapOptions with reason, targetCapability?, errorContext?; swapRuntime() accepts SwapOptions | RerouteOptions via union type; reason defaults to rate_limit_swap for backward compat. buildHandoffDocument sanitizes paneContext, conversationContext, errorContext via sanitize(). failure_reroute adds '## Failure Context' section. SessionHandoff['reason'] in sessions/types.ts needs failure_reroute added separately.

daemon.ts resilience DI pattern: _resilienceStore?: ResilienceStore | null in DaemonOptions; undefined=auto-create from resilience.db, null=disabled, instance=injected. Pair with buildResilienceConfig() to convert OverstoryConfig.resilience (Partial fields) to full ResilienceConfig with defaults.

reroute_recommendation mail fallback (overstory-f804): reroute_recommendation is not in MailProtocolType; use mailClient.send() with type='status' and subject='reroute_recommendation: <taskId>' as structured workaround until protocol type is added.

headroom/types.ts self-contained module pattern (overstory-c3ea): all headroom/quota types (HeadroomState union, HeadroomSnapshot, HeadroomStore, HeadroomConfig) live in src/headroom/types.ts; re-exported via src/types.ts barrel as export type {}; no runtime values

name: Status type hierarchy in overstory
description: 20 distinct status/state types defined across 14 domains (agents, sessions, missions, mail, events, resilience, merge, headroom, runtimes, health, review, recovery, eval, doctor). Organized into 4 categories: Agent Lifecycle (AgentState: booting/working/completed/stalled/zombie; RunStatus: active/completed/failed/stopped; MissionState: active/frozen/completed/failed/stopped/suspended; MissionPhase: understand/align/decide/plan/execute/done), Messaging (MailDeliveryState, MailSemanticType, MailProtocolType, EventLevel, EventType), Infrastructure (CircuitBreakerState, RetryOutcome, ResolutionTier, MergeStatus, HeadroomState, ReadyState), Quality (HealthGrade, ReviewDimension, ReviewSubjectType, ComponentRestoreStatus, ReconciliationStatus). No unified interface; each domain defines independently. JSON output via jsonOutput(command, data) envelope. Document: src/types.ts (barrel), src/*/types.ts (domains), src/commands/{status,health,dashboard}.ts (CLI exposure).

content: health/policy recorder fire-and-forget pattern (overstory-46be): recordPolicyEvent wraps entire body in try/catch so eventStore failures never propagate; suppression dedup checks existingHistory for ruleId+action+suppressed===true before inserting; level is 'warn' for dryRun or suppressed, 'info' for executed

compat/types.ts self-contained module pattern (overstory-0641): all compat types live in src/compat/types.ts — 4 const arrays (SYMBOL_KINDS, SURFACE_CHANGE_KINDS, CHANGE_SEVERITIES, COMPAT_GATE_ACTIONS), 4 derived unions, 6 interfaces (ExportedSymbol, TypeSurface, SurfaceChange, CompatibilityResult, CompatGateDecision, CompatConfig). Barrel re-exports in src/types.ts.

biome noAssignInExpressions fix: while loop using regex exec must initialize before loop: 'let m = re.exec(s); while (m \!== null) { ...; m = re.exec(s); }' instead of 'while ((m = re.exec(s)) \!== null)'

compat gate DI pattern (overstory-a96a): runCompatGate accepts optional _deps={extractSurface, analyze, gitRevParse} for full testability without real git — gitRevParse injectable to avoid Bun.spawn ENOENT in tests; all git operations should be in GateDeps

merge_queue compat_failed migration pattern (overstory-a96a): extending SQLite CHECK constraint requires rebuildTable from db/migrate.ts; add new column via ALTER TABLE first (hasColumn guard), then rebuildTable with updated CREATE_TABLE SQL; bootstrapSchemaVersion detects via new column presence

Config extension pattern: new subsystems define types in src/<domain>/types.ts, import into src/config-types.ts as optional nested fields on OverstoryConfig, provide defaults in DEFAULT_CONFIG (src/config.ts), add validation in validateConfig(). Used by resilience, headroom, mission, compat, reminders, rateLimit subsystems.

deepMerge configuration pattern (src/config-yaml.ts): recursive merge of nested objects, arrays replaced (not appended), undefined source values skipped. Used in loadConfig (src/config.ts:975) to merge file-loaded config over defaults. Enables layered config: defaults + config.yaml + config.local.yaml.

analyze.ts orchestrator pattern (overstory-b39e): use Promise.allSettled for parallel signal analysis — each analyzer independently falls back to a typed default on failure; disabled signals short-circuit with Promise.resolve(default); getValue<T>(result, fallback) helper extracts settled value

context cache.ts readCachedContext sync pattern: function returns ProjectContext | null synchronously using readFileSync from node:fs — checks version===1; writeCachedContext uses Bun.write with JSON.stringify(ctx, null, 2)+'\n' (trailing newline per mx-24764f)

prime.ts export pattern for shared rendering helpers: export renderCompactContext and loadCachedProjectContext from src/commands/prime.ts so spawn.ts and tests can import them without circular deps (agents/ imports from commands/ is fine since commands/ doesn't import from spawn.ts)

config-schema.ts KNOWN_FIELDS.root must be updated when adding new top-level OverstoryConfig fields: adding context to config-types.ts without updating KNOWN_FIELDS.root causes ValidationError on YAML load. Both config-types.ts and config-schema.ts must be updated together.

name: ImportManifest round-trip
description: Manifest CRUD for workflow imports: SHA256 hashes (artifacts + brief content), task mapping by position (workstream ID → source task ID), drift detection via re-hash + re-parse, lazy import avoids circular deps

content: workflow.ts dynamic import fix: createManifest/writeManifest must be static top-level imports, not dynamic import() inside function body

OpenAI consent notice once-per-process pattern (overstory-eef9): module-level 'let openaiNoticeShown = false' flag in semantic.ts prevents repeated stderr writes when embedViaOpenAI called multiple times in same process

Mission graph (DEFAULT_MISSION_GRAPH): 18 nodes (5 working phases × 3 states + 3 terminal), 36 edges. Nodes use 'phase:state' ID format (e.g., 'understand:active'). Terminal nodes (done:completed/stopped/failed) have no outgoing edges. Frozen nodes marked gate='human'. Transitions: freeze/answer (within phase), suspend/resume (pause), phase_advance/handoff (next phase), stop/fail/complete (terminal). Handoff is plan→execute variant with weight:10. Graph structure is declarative, static, pre-computed; Phase 1 is advisory validation only.

Mission reopenCount semantics: incremented atomically in MissionStore.unfreeze() when transitioning frozen→active via 'answer' trigger. Not reset on other state changes. Tracks how many times a mission has cycled through freeze-answer loop. Useful for diagnostics and loop detection. First freeze timestamp stored separately in firstFreezeAt.

MissionStore.completeMission(id) atomically sets state=completed AND completed_at in one SQL UPDATE statement. Ensures no gap where only one field is updated. Pattern: UPDATE with both fields + timestamped updatedAt.

CheckpointStore pattern (overstory-a9b4): checkpoint and transition tables live in sessions.db via MISSION_MIGRATIONS version 2. createCheckpointStore(db) factory takes a shared db connection — called from createMissionStore, exposed as store.checkpoints. getLatestCheckpoint uses ORDER BY rowid DESC (not version DESC) to get insertion-ordered latest across all nodes. saveStepResult wraps checkpoint+transition in db.transaction() for atomicity.

sessions.db shared migration version bump pattern (overstory-a9b4): adding checkpoint tables used MISSION_MIGRATIONS version 2 via ensureMigrations — do NOT manually set PRAGMA user_version. Migration detect() checks sqlite_master for table existence. up() uses CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS for idempotency. Both up() and detect() are required.

MissionGraphNode discriminated union pattern (overstory-17ee): LifecycleGraphNode (kind:'lifecycle', has phase/state/subgraph) and CellGraphNode (kind:'cell', has cellType/gateTimeout/onTimeout). Type guards isLifecycleNode/isCellNode live in missions/types.ts. Cell node IDs validated as starting with cellType+':'. flattenGraph uses visited Set for cycle safety. validateGraph accepts opts.startNodeId to support non-default entry points for subgraph validation.

config-types.ts mission? block extension pattern (overstory-d8bb): add new optional fields to the mission? block in OverstoryConfig, then update KNOWN_FIELDS.mission in config-schema.ts with the new field name as a string. Both changes required together.

observability registry.ts exporter registry pattern (overstory-3e07): createExporterRegistry() returns Map<string, factory>, buildExporters() accepts optional registry override for DI in tests; filters disabled, warns on unknown type

daemon.ts export pipeline DI pattern (overstory-3e07): _exportPipeline?: ExportPipeline | null in DaemonOptions; undefined=build from config, null=disabled for tests; enqueue after session loop using eventStore.getByRun(runId) or getErrors fallback + normalizeSpans

Config system three-tier validation pattern: (1) Unknown field detection on RAW YAML before merging via validateUnknownFields() using KNOWN_FIELDS Sets; (2) Type/range validation on merged config via validateConfig(); (3) Schema migration via config-migrate.ts. Tier 1 catches typos early, Tier 2 enforces constraints, Tier 3 handles backward compat.

Adding new config subsection requires 6-file pattern: (1) Create domain types file (e.g., src/resilience/types.ts); (2) Import type, add optional field to OverstoryConfig in config-types.ts; (3) Add nested KNOWN_FIELDS entries in config-schema.ts for root level + each subsection; (4) Add defaults to DEFAULT_CONFIG in config.ts; (5) Add validation checks in config.ts validateConfig; (6) Add recursive unknown-field checks in config-validate.ts using assertKnownKeys; (7) Add test cases. Example: resilience section with retry/circuitBreaker/reroute subsections.

OverstoryConfig optional sections pattern: add new domain section via (1) define interface in src/<domain>/types.ts, (2) import into config-types.ts, (3) add optional field myfeature?: MyConfig, (4) register in KNOWN_FIELDS.root and KNOWN_FIELDS.<domain>, (5) add recursive validation in config-validate.ts

startPersistentAgent(opts) lifecycle: (1) check existing session, (2) resolve model/runtime from config, (3) deploy hooks via runtime.deployConfig(), (4) create/load identity at .overstory/agents/<name>/, (5) build spawn command, (6) create tmux session, (7) create run or link existing, (8) record session BEFORE beacon (hook-triggered updates need it), (9) wait for TUI ready, (10) send beacon + delayed Enters. Returns {session, runId, pid}.
